lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਿ ਮੇਲੇ ਤਾ ਨਦਰੀ ਆਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: seh mele taa nadaree aaeea |
lines.english: When the Lord causes us to meet the Guru, then we come to see them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਵਡਾ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਜਿਸੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਡੀਆ ਵਡਿਆਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: satigur vaddaa kar saalaaheeai jis vich vaddeea vaddiaaeea |
lines.english: Praise the Great True Guru; within Him is the greatest greatness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਹਥੁ ਧਰਿ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਮਾਰਿ ਕਢੀਆ ਬੁਰਿਆਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: kar hukam masatak hath dhar vichahu maar kadteea buriaaeea |
lines.english: By His Command, when He places His hand on our foreheads, wickedness departs from within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਣਾ ਤਾ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: jaa tis bhaanaa taa man vasaaeea |
lines.english: When it pleases Him, they come to dwell in our minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਹਿਲਾ ਸੁਚਾ ਆਪਿ ਹੋਇ ਸੁਚੈ ਬੈਠਾ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: pahilaa suchaa aap hoe suchai baitthaa aae |
lines.english: First, purifying himself, the Brahmin comes and sits in his purified enclosure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਿ ਤੁਠੈ ਨਉ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਾਈਆ ॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: seh tutthai nau nidh paaeea |18|
lines.english: When the Lord is thoroughly pleased, the nine treasures are obtained. ||18||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਚਾ ਹੋਇ ਕੈ ਜੇਵਿਆ ਲਗਾ ਪੜਣਿ ਸਲੋਕੁ ॥
lines.translit: suchaa hoe kai jeviaa lagaa parran salok |
lines.english: Being purified, he takes his food, and begins to read his sacred verses.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਚੇ ਅਗੈ ਰਖਿਓਨੁ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਭਿਟਿਓ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: suche agai rakhion koe na bhittio jaae |
lines.english: The pure foods, which no one else has touched, are placed before him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਹਥੀ ਜਾਈ ਸਟਿਆ ਕਿਸੁ ਏਹੁ ਲਗਾ ਦੋਖੁ ॥
lines.translit: kuhathee jaaee sattiaa kis ehu lagaa dokh |
lines.english: But it is then thrown into a filthy place - whose fault is this?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਨੁ ਦੇਵਤਾ ਪਾਣੀ ਦੇਵਤਾ ਬੈਸੰਤਰੁ ਦੇਵਤਾ ਲੂਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: an devataa paanee devataa baisantar devataa loon |
lines.english: The corn is sacred, the water is sacred; the fire and salt are sacred as well;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਜਵਾ ਪਾਇਆ ਘਿਰਤੁ ॥ ਤਾ ਹੋਆ ਪਾਕੁ ਪਵਿਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: panjavaa paaeaa ghirat | taa hoaa paak pavit |
lines.english: When the fifth thing, the ghee, is added, then the food becomes pure and sanctified.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਪੀ ਸਿਉ ਤਨੁ ਗਡਿਆ ਥੁਕਾ ਪਈਆ ਤਿਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: paapee siau tan gaddiaa thukaa peea tith |
lines.english: Coming into contact with the sinful human body, the food becomes so impure that is is spat upon.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਊਚਰਹਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਰਸ ਖਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jit mukh naam na aoochareh bin naavai ras khaeh |
lines.english: That mouth which does not chant the Naam, and without the Name eats tasty foods

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਏਵੈ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਤਿਤੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਥੁਕਾ ਪਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak evai jaaneeai tith mukh thukaa paeh |1|
lines.english: - O Nanak, know this: such a mouth is to be spat upon. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੰਡਹੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਦੋਸਤੀ ਭੰਡਹੁ ਚਲੈ ਰਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhanddahu hovai dosatee bhanddahu chalai raahu |
lines.english: Woman becomes his friend; through woman, the future generations come.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੰਡੁ ਮੁਆ ਭੰਡੁ ਭਾਲੀਐ ਭੰਡਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਬੰਧਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhandd muaa bhandd bhaaleeai bhandd hovai bandhaan |
lines.english: When his woman dies, he seeks another woman; to woman he is bound.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੰਡਿ ਜੰਮੀਐ ਭੰਡਿ ਨਿੰਮੀਐ ਭੰਡਿ ਮੰਗਣੁ ਵੀਆਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhandd jameeai bhandd ninmeeai bhandd mangan veeaahu |
lines.english: From woman, man is born; within woman, man is conceived; to woman he is engaged and married.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੰਡਹੁ ਹੀ ਭੰਡੁ ਊਪਜੈ ਭੰਡੈ ਬਾਝੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bhanddahu hee bhandd aoopajai bhanddai baajh na koe |
lines.english: From woman, woman is born; without woman, there would be no one at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਕਿਉ ਮੰਦਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਜਿਤੁ ਜੰਮਹਿ ਰਾਜਾਨ ॥
lines.translit: so kiau mandaa aakheeai jit jameh raajaan |
lines.english: So why call her bad? From her, kings are born.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤੇ ਮੁਖ ਊਜਲੇ ਤਿਤੁ ਸਚੈ ਦਰਬਾਰਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak te mukh aoojale tith sachai darabaar |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, those faces shall be radiant in the Court of the True Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਭੰਡੈ ਬਾਹਰਾ ਏਕੋ ਸਚਾ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak bhanddai baaharaa eko sachaa soe |
lines.english: O Nanak, only the True Lord is without a woman.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਸਦਾ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਭਾਗਾ ਰਤੀ ਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: jit mukh sadaa saalaaheeai bhaagaa ratee chaar |
lines.english: That mouth which praises the Lord continually is blessed and beautiful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਆਖੈ ਆਪਣਾ ਜਿਸੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਸੋ ਚੁਣਿ ਕਢੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: sabh ko aakhai aapanaa jis naahee so chun kadteeai |
lines.english: All call You their own, Lord; one who does not own You, is picked up and thrown away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਰਹਣਾ ਨਾਹੀ ਐਤੁ ਜਗਿ ਤਾ ਕਾਇਤੁ ਗਾਰਬਿ ਹੰਢੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jaa rahanaa naahee ait jag taa kaaeit gaarab handteeai |
lines.english: Since one is not destined to remain in this world anyway, why should he ruin himself in pride?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀਤਾ ਆਪੋ ਆਪਣਾ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਲੇਖਾ ਸੰਢੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: keetaa aapo aapanaa aape hee lekhaa sandteeai |
lines.english: Everyone receives the rewards of his own actions; his account is adjusted accordingly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖੈ ਨਾਲਿ ਨ ਲੁਝੀਐ ॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: moorakhai naal na lujheeai |19|
lines.english: Don't argue with fools. ||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦਾ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਆਖੀਐ ਪੜਿ ਅਖਰੁ ਏਹੋ ਬੁਝੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: mandaa kisai na aakheeai parr akhar eho bujheeai |
lines.english: Do not call anyone bad; read these words, and understand.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਫਿਕੈ ਬੋਲਿਐ ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਫਿਕਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak fikai boliai tan man fikaa hoe |
lines.english: O Nanak, speaking insipid words, the body and mind become insipid.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਿਕਾ ਦਰਗਹ ਸਟੀਐ ਮੁਹਿ ਥੁਕਾ ਫਿਕੇ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: fikaa daragah satteeai muhi thukaa fike paae |
lines.english: The insipid person is discarded in the Court of the Lord, and the insipid one's face is spat upon.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਿਕੋ ਫਿਕਾ ਸਦੀਐ ਫਿਕੇ ਫਿਕੀ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: fiko fikaa sadeeai fike fikee soe |
lines.english: He is called the most insipid of the insipid; the most insipid of the insipid is his reputation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਿਕਾ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਪਾਣਾ ਲਹੈ ਸਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: fikaa moorakh aakheeai paanaa lahai sajaae |1|
lines.english: The insipid one is called a fool; he is beaten with shoes in punishment. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਹੁ ਝੂਠੇ ਪੈਜ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਫੈਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: andarahu jhootthe paij baahar duneea andar fail |
lines.english: Those who are false within, and honorable on the outside, are very common in this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਜੇ ਨਾਵਹਿ ਉਤਰੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਮੈਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: atthasatth teerath je naaveh utarai naahee mail |
lines.english: Even though they may bathe at the sixty-eight sacred shrines of pilgrimage, still, their filth does not depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑ ਪਟੁ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਗੁਦੜੁ ਤੇ ਭਲੇ ਸੰਸਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: jina patt andar baahar gudarr te bhale sansaar |
lines.english: Those who have silk on the inside and rags on the outside, are the good ones in this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨੑ ਨੇਹੁ ਲਗਾ ਰਬ ਸੇਤੀ ਦੇਖਨੑੇ ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: tina nehu lagaa rab setee dekhanae veechaar |
lines.english: They embrace love for the Lord, and contemplate beholding Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੰਗਿ ਹਸਹਿ ਰੰਗਿ ਰੋਵਹਿ ਚੁਪ ਭੀ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: rang haseh rang roveh chup bhee kar jaeh |
lines.english: In the Lord's Love, they laugh, and in the Lord's Love, they weep, and also keep silent.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਿ ਵਾਟ ਉਪਰਿ ਖਰਚੁ ਮੰਗਾ ਜਬੈ ਦੇਇ ਤ ਖਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: dar vaatt upar kharach mangaa jabai dee ta khaeh |
lines.english: Sitting, waiting at the Lord's Door, they beg for food, and when He gives to them, they eat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਵਾਹ ਨਾਹੀ ਕਿਸੈ ਕੇਰੀ ਬਾਝੁ ਸਚੇ ਨਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: paravaah naahee kisai keree baajh sache naah |
lines.english: They do not care for anything else, except their True Husband Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੀਬਾਨੁ ਏਕੋ ਕਲਮ ਏਕਾ ਹਮਾ ਤੁਮੑਾ ਮੇਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: deebaan eko kalam ekaa hamaa tumaa mel |
lines.english: There is only One Court of the Lord, and He has only one pen; there, you and I shall meet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਿ ਲਏ ਲੇਖਾ ਪੀੜਿ ਛੁਟੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਜਿਉ ਤੇਲੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: dar le lekhaa peerr chhuttai naanakaa jiau tel |2|
lines.english: In the Court of the Lord, the accounts are examined; O Nanak, the sinners are crushed, like oil seeds in the press. ||2||
